payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Task claiming by children
payload.justification: Claiming represents the strategic process by which children record completion of tasks.

payload.description: Task definition by parents
payload.justification: Task definition is the strategic process parents use to create work items with rewards.

payload.description: Parental review of earnings and completion
payload.justification: Review process enables parents to verify task completion before disbursement.

payload.description: Earnings disbursement to children
payload.justification: Disbursement is the implied payment process following parental review.

payload.description: Pocket money task
payload.justification: Tasks are the core entity representing work items with monetary value that children complete and parents define.

payload.description: Weekly earnings total
payload.justification: Earnings total is a distinct strategic entity representing accumulated monetary value from completed tasks.

payload.description: Monetary reward associated with task
payload.justification: Monetary reward is a defining attribute of pocket money tasks.

payload.description: Task completion status
payload.justification: Completion status distinguishes available from completed tasks and is central to claiming and review.

payload.description: Weekly earnings cycle
payload.justification: The week represents the strategic temporal cycle governing task claiming and earnings totaling.

payload.description: Week end occurrence
payload.justification: Week end is the temporal event when earnings totals become visible to children.

payload.description: Child user
payload.justification: Children are distinct actors who claim tasks, view availability, and see earnings.

payload.description: Parent user
payload.justification: Parents are distinct actors who define tasks and review completion for payment.

payload.description: Enable children to record completed pocket money work
payload.justification: Core strategic goal of empowering children to claim their completed tasks.

payload.description: Provide task and earnings visibility to children
payload.justification: Strategic goal of transparency enabling children to see available tasks and accumulated earnings.

payload.description: Enable parental control of task rewards and payment verification
payload.justification: Strategic goal empowering parents to define incentives and verify work before payment.

payload.description: Report on child-earnings and task-completion relationships
payload.justification: SG926 establishes parent reporting capability as a business function supporting payment fulfillment.

payload.description: Define tasks with monetary values
payload.justification: SG922 describes parent capability to define task entities with monetary attributes as a business function.

payload.description: View available and personally-completed tasks within current week
payload.justification: SG924 specifies child visibility over task entities as a business function requirement.

payload.description: Task availability status cycles at weekly temporal boundaries
payload.justification: SG927 defines the weekly reset as a business rule governing task availability cycling.

payload.description: Task completion data must persist beyond current operational week
payload.justification: SG928 establishes temporal persistence as a business rule requirement.

payload.description: Child-earnings relationship
payload.justification: SG926 explicitly mentions child-earnings relationship as reportable business information.

payload.description: Task-completion relationship
payload.justification: SG926 explicitly mentions task-completion relationship as reportable business information.

payload.description: Task
payload.justification: Task is described as a core business entity across multiple signals including definition, completion linkage, visibility, and availability status.

payload.description: Monetary value of task
payload.justification: SG922 describes monetary value as an attribute associated with task entities.

payload.description: Child
payload.justification: Child is described as a business entity with relationships to tasks, visibility requirements, and earnings aggregation.

payload.description: Completed task linkage to child upon completion
payload.justification: SG923 establishes the conceptual relationship between completed task entities and child entities.

payload.description: Weekly earnings
payload.justification: SG925 defines weekly earnings aggregation as a derived business entity.

payload.description: Task availability status
payload.justification: SG927 describes availability status as an attribute of task that cycles at temporal boundaries.

payload.description: Weekly operational cycle
payload.justification: Weekly cycle is described across multiple signals as the fundamental temporal period for task tracking and earnings aggregation.

payload.description: Week-end temporal boundary
payload.justification: Week-end boundary is described as a significant temporal event when earnings become visible and task availability resets.

payload.description: Task completion event
payload.justification: SG923 implies task completion as a triggering event that establishes linkage between task and child entities.

payload.description: Parent
payload.justification: Parent is described as a business actor with capabilities to define tasks and access reporting.

payload.description: Child user
payload.justification: SG924 describes child as an actor with specific visibility requirements over task entities.

payload.description: Track child completion of tasks and associated pocket money claiming throughout weekly cycles
payload.justification: SG921 explicitly defines the core business purpose of the pocket money tracker system.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL CellContentItem elements present in the ledger.

payload.name: Family Participation
payload.description: Defines the enterprise accountability for establishing and maintaining the parent-child relationship roles through which household responsibility, delegation, and oversight are expressed.

payload.name: Household Work Obligation
payload.description: Establishes the enterprise commitment to defining compensable household work units, their associated monetary value, completion criteria, and the periodic earnings structure that governs child compensation.

payload.name: Delegation and Oversight Authority
payload.description: Governs the enterprise mechanisms through which authority to assign work is delegated, work commitments are accepted, completion is verified, and approval authority over disbursements is retained by senior stakeholders.

payload.name: Stakeholder Transparency
payload.description: Ensures the enterprise obligation to provide visibility into work obligations, completion status, and earned compensation to participating stakeholders, supporting informed decision-making and accountability.

payload.name: Task Stewardship
payload.description: Business responsibility for defining and governing the catalog of compensated tasks, including monetary valuation and availability criteria that determine when tasks may be undertaken within operational cycles.

payload.name: Completion Accountability
payload.description: Business responsibility for establishing and preserving the record of task completions attributed to specific children, maintaining integrity of completion claims across temporal boundaries and operational cycles.

payload.name: Earnings Entitlement
payload.description: Business responsibility for determining and recording the monetary entitlements accrued by children through completed tasks within weekly operational cycles, establishing the relationship between effort and compensation.

payload.name: Participation Oversight
payload.description: Business responsibility for providing visibility into task opportunities and completion status, enabling participants to understand available obligations and review completion records within current operational contexts.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise child user as a participant role.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise's commitment to recognising the child user as a distinct role within the family participation structure. This is a structural obligation defining a participant entity at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise parent user as a participant role.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise's commitment to recognising the parent user as a distinct role within the family participation structure. This is a structural obligation defining a participant entity at enterprise scope.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish household work units eligible for child compensation.
payload.rationale: The enterprise commits to defining the scope of compensable household work as discrete tasks.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain a monetary value for each compensable household work unit.
payload.rationale: Each task entity carries an associated monetary reward attribute that the enterprise must establish and maintain.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish completion criteria for household work units.
payload.rationale: The enterprise must define what constitutes a completed task to enable determination of compensation eligibility.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide aggregate child earnings on a weekly cycle.
payload.rationale: The enterprise commits to calculating and presenting total compensation at week-end occurrence, establishing the temporal structure of the earnings obligation.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish mechanisms by which children may claim tasks defined by parents.
payload.rationale: CCIs describe a bidirectional enterprise process: parents define tasks and children claim them. This is a single delegation capability enabling enterprise task assignment.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain oversight of task completion and associated earnings through parental review.
payload.rationale: Parental review of earnings and completion represents an enterprise accountability mechanism for verifying delegated work.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall retain authority over the disbursement of earnings to children.
payload.rationale: Disbursement authority is retained at the parental level, representing an enterprise governance constraint aligned with the goal of parental control over rewards and payment verification.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable participating stakeholders to record completed work obligations.
payload.rationale: Derived from the enterprise goal to enable children to record completed pocket money work. At Row 1, this is expressed as the enterprise commitment to provide the capability for stakeholders to record work completion, without specifying system mechanics.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide visibility into work obligations and earned compensation to participating stakeholders.
payload.rationale: Derived from the enterprise goal to provide task and earnings visibility. This expresses the enterprise commitment to transparency regarding work obligations and compensation, supporting the stakeholder transparency domain.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a catalog of compensated tasks.
payload.rationale: HOW-column capability to define tasks combined with WHAT-column Task entity establishes the business responsibility to maintain the catalog.

payload.statement: The business shall record a monetary value for each compensated task.
payload.rationale: HOW-column capability to define tasks with monetary values combined with WHAT-column attribute drives the obligation to record valuation.

payload.statement: Each task shall have an availability status.
payload.rationale: WHAT-column attribute establishes a structural composition requirement for the Task entity.

payload.statement: The business shall govern task availability status transitions at weekly temporal boundaries.
payload.rationale: HOW-column business rule constrains when availability status may cycle, establishing a temporal governance obligation.
payload.fit_criteria: Availability status transitions occur only at weekly boundary events.

payload.statement: The parent shall be accountable for defining compensated tasks.
payload.rationale: WHO-column Parent actor combined with HOW-column task definition capability establishes the business role accountability.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of task completion data beyond the current operational week.
payload.rationale: How-column business rule establishes a temporal constraint on the business's accountability for completion records, ensuring continuity across operational cycles.
payload.fit_criteria: Task completion records remain accessible for verification beyond the boundary of the operational week in which they were recorded.

payload.statement: The business shall record the completion of each task.
payload.rationale: What-column relationship (task-completion) combined with When-column trigger (task completion event) establishes the business capability to capture and maintain completion records at the point of completion.

payload.statement: The business shall attribute each completed task to the child who completed it.
payload.rationale: What-column relationship (linkage to child upon completion) combined with Who-column actor establishes the structural business requirement that each completion record carries an association to a specific child participant.

payload.statement: The business shall maintain a record of each child's earnings.
payload.rationale: The child-earnings relationship (CCI-ROW2-C-What-001), the Child entity (CCI-ROW2-C-What-005), and Weekly earnings entity (CCI-ROW2-C-What-007) together establish the business responsibility to maintain a record linking children to their monetary entitlements.

payload.statement: Weekly earnings shall be associated with a child.
payload.rationale: The Weekly earnings entity and the child-earnings relationship impose a structural obligation that earnings records must belong to a specific child.

payload.statement: The business shall account for earnings entitlement within weekly operational cycles.
payload.rationale: The weekly operational cycle (CCI-ROW2-C-When-001) and week-end temporal boundary (CCI-ROW2-C-When-002) establish the business responsibility to determine and record earnings on a weekly basis.

payload.statement: The business shall be accountable for tracking task completion and associated claiming throughout weekly cycles.
payload.rationale: The business goal (CCI-ROW2-C-Why-001) expresses the capability to monitor child task completion and the claiming of pocket money across weekly operational periods.

payload.statement: The business shall make available a record of each child user's completed tasks and associated earnings.
payload.rationale: How-column capability to report on child-earnings and task-completion relationships establishes a business responsibility to make these records available to the accountable role (Parent). The business must maintain visibility into the relationship between work performed and compensation.

payload.statement: The business shall make available to each child user a record of task opportunities and completion status within the current operational week.
payload.rationale: How-column capability to view available and personally-completed tasks establishes a business responsibility to provide visibility into current-week task opportunities and completion records for the accountable role (Child user). The temporal boundary (current week) is part of the operational context stated in the source CCI.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Requirement elements present in the ledger.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Establishes the core purpose of enabling children to claim completed pocket money tasks throughout the week.
Articulates parental motivation to define tasks with associated monetary rewards.
Expresses the intent to provide children visibility into available and completed tasks.
States the purpose of enabling children to see weekly earnings totals at week end.
Articulates parental need to review child earnings and task completion for disbursement purposes.
Identifies the section boundary introducing conceptual overview content for the pocket money tracker domain.
Defines the core business purpose as tracking child completion of tasks and associated pocket money claiming throughout weekly cycles.
Describes the parent actor capability to define task entities with associated monetary value attributes.
Establishes the conceptual relationship between completed task entities and child entities through linkage upon completion.
Specifies child actor visibility requirements over both available and personally-completed task entities within the current week context.
Defines the weekly earnings aggregation concept as a derived business entity visible to children at week-end temporal boundaries.
Establishes parent reporting capability over child-earnings and task-completion relationships to support payment fulfillment.
Defines the weekly reset business rule where task availability status cycles at temporal week boundaries.
Establishes requirement for temporal persistence of task completion data beyond current operational week.
